summary: DHH announces that Rails beta releases are now available as Ruby gems, eliminating the need to use CVS (the version control system) to access the latest features. Previously, developers who wanted cutting-edge Rails had to use 'Edge Rails' via CVS, sacrificing the convenience of gem-based package management. With help from Chad Fowler, DHH set up a gem server for beta distributions. This is a brief, practical announcement about Rails infrastructure improvements.
key_points: Rails beta releases are now available via a gem server, not just CVS
Edge Rails (CVS version) was previously the only way to get the latest features
Chad Fowler helped set up the beta gem server infrastructure
This bridges the gap between cutting-edge access and package management convenience
money_quotes: Until now, you've been restricted to the CVS version if you wanted the latest and greatest, which meant giving up the goodness of gems. Those days are over.
key_insight: Making Rails beta releases available as gems removed a key friction point for developers who wanted both cutting-edge features and proper package management.
